learning_outcomes.description: Apply reflective practice to identify goals and self-evaluate progress in ongoing resilience and professional development, appropriately selecting and utilising healthcare evidence and feedback to ensure capability for practice in multiple settings.

As a developing student, act with initiative and judgement in planning, problem solving and decision making in an interprofessional practice setting, adhering to requirements defined by Nursing and Midwifery Board of Australia's Professional Standards.
Utilise effective communication skills to make a clear and coherent presentation of knowledge and ideas with intellectual independence.

Commence to effectively plan, implement and evaluate midwifery care that respects and acknowledges the values of women, neonates and their families of different cultures and backgrounds, while maintaining the principles of confidentiality.
Demonstrate and consolidate effective time management, clinical reasoning, and professional judgement to deliver woman centred care, that can be utilised in global environments.

assessments.due_date: MedSafe calculation certificates by end of week 1 and Midwifery Clinical Skills x2 to be completed by mid-point AMSAT. An expectation of MPE progression will also occur at the Mid-point AMSAT.
assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Performance, Placement, Portfolio, Quiz, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
This assessment task comprises of three (3) parts which will examine and extend your preparedness for clinical placement and development in the first half of the semester.

Part 1 – Medication safety

This assessment task requires the student to undertake an online assessment on medication safety as well as completion of a drug calculations assessment.

Students must receive a 100% pass mark to be able to pass this assessment. Further instructions can be found in the assessment task and via the BlackBoard site.

Part 2 - Skill Assessment

This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. Two (2) skills should be completed by the time of the Mid-Point AMSAT.

Part 3 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW2109 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT)

The Mid-point AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife.

Completion due date is mid placement (approx. 80 hours of the student's placement) - complete all Standards

For all parts, please access the task description in the blackboard site for more information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: MedSafe certificate submitted to the companion Blackboard site under Assessments. All MPEs, CoCEs and AMSAT to be submitted to ePAD and verified by a registered practitioner.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date: All required Midwifery Clinical Skills and final AMSAT, including MPEs, hours and recommended CoCEs need to be submitted by the completion of clinical placement.
assessments.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Performance, Placement, Portfolio, Reflection
assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to demonstrate successful completion of clinical placement. There are three (3) parts to this assessment:

Part 1 - Skill Assessment

This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice, and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. To complete three (3) skills and present at time of the Final AMSAT.

Part 2 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW2109 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT)

The Final AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife.

Completion due date is at the end of your placement (around 160 hours) - complete all Standards

Part 3 - Completion and upload of required Midwifery Practice Experiences (MPE) including Continuity of Care Experiences (CoCE).

This assessment requires the student to upload at least 60 MPE and recommended to recruit at least four (4) CoCE.

For all parts, please access the task description in the Blackboard site for more information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All required clinical skills, MPE's, CoCEs and AMSATs need to be submitted to ePAD and verified by a registered practitioner.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Exam (School) - during Exam Period: Midwifery OSCE
assessments.due_date: Students will be allocated a time during the Examination period.
assessments.category: Examination, Practical/ Demonstration, Role play/ Simulation
assessments.task_description:
This practical exam will require the student to demonstrate, theoretical knowledge, clinical confidence and competence in demonstration of skills chosen from the course content of MIDW2109. 

This examination will be conducted in a simulated environment and will consist of one case which will require performance of two [2] different clinical skills. Following skills performance students will be asked two [2] questions to explain underpinning theory content knowledge for the skill. For each of the two [2] skills and questions, assessed students will be marked against the quality of a performance rubric.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
MIDW2109 Clinical Practice 4a Placement

Clinical practice attendance at allocated hospital/location. Details of dates & times will vary depending on placement site. Further information can be obtained from the practice education team and the Blackboard site HABS0836_7500_00001.

Completion of all clinical practice shifts (160hrs) is compulsory.

Compliance Statement

It is expected that students have successfully completed all requirements for this course. Students are responsible for meeting all pre-placement mandatory requirements before the start of the semester. Failure to comply with these requirements will result in no clinical placement allocation. Students who receive written notification of failure to comply with pre-placement and mandatory requirements will be required to address these by the end of week 3 of the semester. If students do not meet the final compliance deadlines by the end of week 3, they will no longer be eligible to undertaken clinical placement and no further clinical placement allocation will occur. In these instances students are advised to withdraw from the course before the census date.

Unsafe practice during clinical placement:

A decision of unsafe clinical performance is made when a student's performance places a woman/person/family, staff members, themselves, or fellow students at risk. If students practice in a way that is deemed unsafe or breaches the relevant scope of practice during their clinical placement they will be asked to leave the placement and a meet with the Course Coordinator and Program Lead.

Professional manner during clinical placement:

Students must always conduct themselves professionally during professional placement. Unprofessional behaviour may lead to a learning support plan, immediate removal from the clinical placement, or termination of the placement. This could result in non-academic misconduct and a failing grade in the associated subject.

Scope of Practice

Students must adhere to the course Scope of Practice at all times while on clinical placement. This requirement addresses the skills appropriate to perform safely and students will be removed from placement or risk failing the course if working outside their scope. Please refer to the guidelines in the Nursing & Midwifery Practice Education (Clinical Placements) Blackboard site (HABS0836).

Social Media

Students are prohibited from posting any images of patients, clinical venues, or clinical documentation on social media. Students should also refrain from making comments related to clinical placement, UQ, or clinical venues that may violate University or institutional policies or guidelines or bring UQ into disrepute. Failure to comply with this policy will result in removal of the student(s) from placement, and/or a failing grade for this course.

See guidelines section below for links to appropriate documents.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Introductory Lecture

This lecture will identify the requirements in the course, including CoCE, MPE and clinical based learning class specifics. All associated assessments will be discussed, including MedSafe and the OSCE.



Learning outcomes: L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Introductory Lecture This lecture will identify the requirements in the course, including CoCE, MPE and clinical based learning class specifics. All associated assessments will be discussed, including MedSafe and the OSCE.

learning_activities.topic:
Clinical Based Learning (CBL/PRA) Workshops

These clinical simulated learning sessions will be held on alternate weeks 2,4,6,8,10,12. ATTENDANCE IS COMPULSORY.

Learning is supported by online modules to prepare for class. Simulation will include role play and augmented reality with skill development to support students manage midwifery and obstetric emergencies, such as Breech Birth, Shoulder Dystocia, Postpartum Haemorrhage, Eclampsia, Maternal & Neonatal Resuscitation.

In the timetable these sessions are displayed as PRA.


Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
